Mtn To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-09-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DAVID FUJIMOTO, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld, APC (tel: 213-380-2344) for Plaintiff Gregory Roberts.
Atty. KEVIN LEE, Esq. of Jackson Lewis P.C. (415-394-9400) for Defendants Redmint, LLC, Redmint Acupuncture, Inc. and Helina Ying-Fan Au.
Atty. JESSICA COLLINS, Esq. of Fisher & Phillips LLP (tel: 415-490-9000) for Defendant Trinet HR III, Inc.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT REDMINT ONE, LLC's Mtn To Compel Arbitration.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Redmint One, LLC's motion to compel arbitration and stay is denied.

Plaintiff pleads a claim for sexual harassment and therefore the action is not arbitrable under the federal Ending Forced Arbitration of Sexual Assault and Sexual Harassment Act ("EFAA").

The EFAA voids a pre-dispute arbitration agreement "with respect to a case which . . .relates to . . . the sexual harassment dispute." (9 U.S.C. 402(a) (emphasis added).)

Here, plaintiff pleads a cognizable claim for sexual harassment. The court liberally construes plaintiff's complaint per CCP 452. In 2019, the California Legislature clarified harassment law and noted "[a] single incident of harassing conduct is sufficient to create a triable issue regarding the existence of a hostile work environment if the harassing conduct has unreasonably interfered with the plaintiff's work performance or created an intimidating, hostile, or offensive work environment." (Government Code section 12923(b); see also Beltran v. Hard Rock Hotel Licensing, Inc. (2023) 97 Cal.App.5th 865, 878-880 [noting less demanding standard and inapplicability of former cases]; CACI 2524 [defining severe or pervasive]; Bailey v. San Francisco District Attorney's Office (2024) 16 Cal.5th 611 [use of single racial epithet could constitute severe conduct to support harassment claim].)

Plaintiff alleges several specific instances of objectionable conduct and notes that the conduct spanned "at least an eight-month period." (Complaint, pars. 21-24, 38.) Plaintiff alleges a viable sexual harassment claim and defendant largely relies on outdated cases to argue otherwise.

As alluded to above, the case is exempt from arbitration. Thus, there is no basis to sever plaintiff's non-harassment claims and compel them to arbitration. (See Turner v. Tesla, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2023) 686 F.Supp.3d 917, 925 ["[T]he text of Sec. 402(a) makes clear that its invalidation of an arbitration agreement extends to the entirety of the case"].)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
